summary: Backend-only testing for profile redesign (postsCount/likesCount + private friends list) and tournament redesign (room-scoped, public/private, tiered prize split). 17/17 pytest tests passed including unit-level checks on _winners_count, _prize_split, _generate_join_code, and the TOURNAMENT_VISIBLE_HOURS=5 constant, plus end-to-end flows hitting the public preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is now 4118 lines — strongly recommend splitting into modules (auth, tournaments, posts, profile, rooms). Maintenance/readability risk.
Tournament join-by-code matches only status=='lobby'; that's correct behavior. Confirmed no _id leakage in serialized responses.

_prize_split with k>=3 uses ratios n:(n-1):...:1 where n is the winners_count (k), not n_players. Tests confirmed this matches the spec given in the request ('ceil(0.3*n) winners with ratios k:(k-1):...:1' where the comment in code says 'k'). For 11 players → 4 winners → [4,3,2,1] weights → pot 110 → [44,33,22,11]. Matches expected.
list_room_tournaments performs an in-Python loop to filter private tournaments — fine at small scale but could be pushed to the Mongo query for efficiency at higher volume.
Endpoint name discrepancy: review_request mentions POST /api/board-posts/{id}/like — actual endpoint is POST /api/posts/{post_id}/like. Tests used the actual endpoint.
test_credentials: Fresh users registered per-test (alice*, bob*, carol*, p0..p6, q0..q9). Pre-seeded users in /app/memory/test_credentials.md were not used — module-scoped fixtures create fresh accounts.
seed_data_creation: Called POST /api/init/rooms (idempotent) to seed default rooms. ~20 throwaway TEST users created across the suite (alice/bob/carol + p0-p6 + q0-q9). One board post + one like persisted in default room A by Alice→Bob.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend tournament + profile changes verified. Helper functions _winners_count/_prize_split are pure and importable from /app/backend/server.py. If you re-run, the test suite registers fresh users each run so it is safe to run repeatedly. Tournament auto-completion on size-fill works; manual /start with <size players also works for ≤4 tier.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. All assertions on status codes, response shapes, privacy gating (403 friends + private tournament join), join-code format (6 chars from A-Z\{I,O} + 2-9), room scoping, and tiered prize math passed.
